name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained. Repeated, automated login attempts against a single account are a common signature.

name: Password Guessing
description: Adversaries with no prior knowledge of legitimate credentials may guess passwords to attempt access to accounts, often using a small list of common or default passwords against one account.

name: Password Spraying
description: Adversaries may use a single or small list of commonly used passwords against many different accounts to attempt to acquire valid account credentials while avoiding account lockouts from a per-account brute force attempt.

name: Valid Accounts
tactic: Defense Evasion, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Initial Access
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining access. Compromised credentials may be used to bypass access controls and blend in with normal user activity, making detection difficult.

name: Remote Services: SSH
description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a machine using Secure Shell (SSH). SSH is a protocol that allows authorized users to open remote shells on other systems.

name: Off-Hours Access Anomaly
description: Successful authentication outside of an account's normal working hours is not itself an ATT&CK technique, but is a widely used behavioral analytic for prioritizing which Valid Accounts activity to review first.
